what_it_does: Acceptance test for the Convergence X8 deduplication fix in Suite._r2_gather: it verifies that a single operator comment ingested through ingest_comment, which surfaces as an annotation stratum, a chat turn, and a truncated addressed event echo, collapses to one gathered item keyed by (address, underlying text), with the full-text survivor winning; it also verifies distinct comments are not over-collapsed, the R2 budget cap and ranking remain unchanged, non-comment addressed events are preserved, and the production _resolve_context_at path still resolves cleanly.
observations: The test is organized into four parts: single-comment dedup, distinct-comment preservation, an adversarial >40-char shared-prefix case, and preservation of cap/ranking/non-comment events/end-to-end resolve.
It creates multiple Suite instances backed by temporary FsStore directories under a single mkdtemp root.
It directly calls internal/private methods: _r2_gather, _r2_score_and_cap, and _resolve_context_at.
It uses a module-level PASS counter and a check helper that asserts and prints per-check.
It defines a nested carries predicate to detect gathered items that contain the comment text or its 40-char prefix.
It constructs a long comment >40 chars to exercise the event-echo truncation path.
For the cap test it directly manipulates store4.append_annotation to inject many distinct annotations.
It asserts the survivor kind is annotation or chat, not event, and that the resolved context block contains the comment exactly once.
standouts: The test encodes a specific defect narrative and proof model in its docstring and assertions.
It tests a dedup identity that is robust to 40-character truncation by dropping an event echo only when its text is a prefix of a kept full-text item at the same address.
It includes an adversarial case of two comments sharing a >40-char common prefix to guard against over-collapse.
conventions: Uses module-level constants ROOT, PASS, NODES, LOCUS, PARENT.
Uses sys.path insertion and the COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment flag.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp with shutil.rmtree cleanup in a try/finally block.
Uses descriptive uppercase fixture strings for comments.
Uses a check helper with a global counter for pass tally.
concerns: The test reaches into private implementation details (_r2_gather, _r2_score_and_cap, _resolve_context_at), making it brittle to refactoring.
It directly manipulates store4.append_annotation, bypassing public ingest/attach APIs for part of the cap test.
The global PASS counter and check helper mix assertion side effects with printed output.
Some assertions assume specific formatting such as a '[comment @' prefix and the literal string 'CONTEXT RESOLVED AT YOUR LOCUS', coupling them to prompt templates.
notes: This is a tightly-scoped acceptance test that proves a deduplication fix for comment echoes in the R2 gather, with explicit guards against truncation-driven over-collapse.
questions: What exact shape/fields distinguish a 'comment echo' event from other addressed events?
How is the dedup key computed in _r2_gather for annotation vs chat vs event items?
What is the value of R2_BUDGET and how does _r2_score_and_cap rank and truncate items?
Are there other ingest paths besides ingest_comment that could produce the same triple-stratum pattern?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it validates the X8 dedup fix, the truncation-safe identity, preservation of cap/ranking, non-comment event survival, and end-to-end context resolution.
apparent_themes: Regression acceptance testing with adversarial fixtures.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock in a fix that prevents a single user comment from consuming multiple R2 budget slots due to annotation/chat/event echo duplication, while ensuring genuinely distinct comments remain separate and the existing cap/ranking behavior is untouched.
novelty: It formalizes a truncation-robust dedup identity and an adversarial shared-prefix test, which is a deliberate refinement beyond naive full-text or prefix-key deduplication.
contribution: It provides an acceptance test that documents and guards the X8 dedup behavior for the R2 gather, including the truncation-safe collapse rule, distinct-comment preservation, cap/ranking invariants, and end-to-end context resolution.
summary_for_embedding: tests/conv_dedup_acceptance.py is an acceptance test for the Convergence X8 R2-gather deduplication fix, verifying that a single ingested comment appearing as annotation, chat, and truncated event echo collapses to one full-text item, distinct comments are preserved, and the budget cap and ranking remain intact.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to prove that the deduplication of comment strata in Suite._r2_gather is robust to 40-character event truncation and does not over-collapse distinct comments or alter existing R2 budget behavior.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: A test helper that takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL message, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints an ok line; it is used throughout the acceptance test to tally individual checks.

symbols.does: Returns whether a gathered item contains the full comment text or its 40-character prefix.
symbols.description: A nested predicate used in Part 1 to identify gathered items that represent the clicked comment in any of its wrapped forms, matching either the complete comment string or the truncated event-echo prefix.
